provenance.generator_model_id: text-only run, no images generated by design

project.name: Entity Reference Verification Assessment
project.task_type: Same-entity verification of a boxed target region against a reference, six verdicts plus a feature-named reason.

settings.house_style: no em dashes, no semicolons, no emojis

projects.name: entity-reference-verification
projects.sop_title: Entity Reference Verification - Standard Operating Procedure

inputs.instruction: Entity type: person. Label: man at cafe counter. Verify whether the yellow-boxed face on the target and the green-boxed face on the reference are the same person.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A man photographed indoors in a red jacket, yellow box around his face. Square jaw, short dark beard, brown eyes, side-parted dark hair, small mole on left cheek.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The same man outdoors in a blue hoodie, green box around his face. Identical jaw, beard, eye color, side-parted hair, same mole on left cheek. Only outfit and background differ.

verdict.reason: For a person, identity is the face, not the outfit. Face structure, beard, eye color, hair part, and the left-cheek mole all match and are clearly visible, so the call is Definitely (SOP 4.1, 8.1).

checklist: Names at least two specific facial identity features such as jaw or beard or eyes or hair part or the mole
Anchors the decision on the face, not the outfit or background
States the features match
constraints: Reason must name concrete facial features, not a generic phrase like same person (SOP 5.4)
Reason must not use outfit or background as the deciding signal (SOP 4.1)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names two or more matching facial features and does not rely on outfit or background.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: person. Label: woman on train platform. Verify whether the yellow-boxed face on the target and the green-boxed face on the reference are the same person.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A woman at dusk on a platform, yellow box around her face which is turned three-quarters away and lit only by a dim overhead lamp. Features are in heavy shadow, outline of nose and chin visible but fine detail is not.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A clear daylight headshot of a woman, green box around a fully lit front-facing face. Plausibly the same person but the target is too dark to confirm specific features.

verdict.reason: The target face is in heavy shadow and turned away, so no identifying feature is clearly visible. The images load fine and the annotation is legitimate, so this is a Skip, not a Flag, and rating Definitely Same here would be a 2-star miscalibration (SOP 5.2, 5.3).

checklist: States that the target face cannot be confidently read due to shadow, angle, or distance
Explains why this is a Skip rather than a confident verdict
Does not claim a match or conflict that the visible detail cannot support
constraints: Reason must justify a Skip by naming the visibility problem, not by guessing identity (SOP 5.2)
Reason must not assert matching features that are not clearly visible (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason ties the Skip to insufficient visible facial detail and avoids asserting an unsupported match.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: clothing. Label: black hoodie with chest logo. Verify whether the yellow-boxed garment on the target and the green-boxed garment on the reference are the same garment.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A black pullover hoodie worn by a model, yellow box around the garment. Small white circular logo sits high on the left chest, about 10 cm below the shoulder seam. Flat drawcords, kangaroo pocket.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A black pullover hoodie of the same cut and color, green box around the garment. The same white circular logo is centered low on the chest near the pocket seam, clearly a different placement. Same brand styling otherwise.

verdict.reason: Logo placement is a garment-identifying construction feature and it clearly conflicts, high on the chest versus low near the pocket. Both are clearly visible, so the call is Definitely Different (SOP 4.2, 7 Q2, 11.1).

checklist: Names logo placement as the conflicting feature
States the two placements differ between target and reference
Treats placement as a garment-construction signal rather than relying on the model or color
constraints: Reason must name a garment-specific construction feature, not the person or a generic same jacket phrase (SOP 4.2, 5.4)
Reason must identify the conflict that drives a Different verdict (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names the conflicting logo placement and treats it as a garment-construction feature.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: clothing. Label: red satin slip dress. Verify whether the yellow-boxed garment on the target and the green-boxed garment on the reference are the same garment.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A model in a red satin slip dress, yellow box around the dress. Thin spaghetti straps about 5 mm wide, plain straight neckline, knee length.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The same model in a red satin slip dress of the same color and length, green box around the dress. Straps are wider, about 1 inch, and a small bow sits at the center of the neckline. Same fabric and silhouette otherwise.

verdict.reason: Strap width and the neckline bow are garment-specific construction details that clearly differ. Same model, color, fabric, and silhouette do not establish identity for clothing (SOP 4.2 Think First). Both differing features are clearly visible, so Definitely Different.

checklist: Names strap width or the neckline bow as a conflicting construction detail
States the construction differs even though color, fabric, or model match
Does not treat the matching model or color as evidence of same garment
constraints: Reason must locate identity in garment construction, not in the person or the shared color (SOP 4.2)
Reason must name the concrete differing detail (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names a differing construction detail such as strap width or the bow and does not lean on the shared model or color.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: product. Label: cereal box. Verify whether the yellow-boxed product on the target and the green-boxed product on the reference are the same product.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A cereal box, yellow box around it. Same brand mascot and logo, front banner reads Original. Box is photographed facing forward.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A cereal box of the same brand and box shape, green box around it. Identical mascot and logo, but the front banner reads Honey Nut and the background color is yellow rather than red. One clear line of variant text differs.

verdict.reason: Same model but a different variant. The variant text Original versus Honey Nut and the different colorway make this a different product, even though brand and box shape match (SOP 4.3, 8.9). The differing line of text is clearly visible, so Definitely Different.

checklist: Names the variant text or colorway as the conflicting feature
States that a different variant of the same model is a different product
Does not treat the shared brand or box shape as proof of same product
constraints: Reason must name packaging variant text or colorway, not a generic same product phrase (SOP 4.3, 5.4)
Reason must justify Different by the variant conflict (SOP 4.3)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names the differing variant text or colorway and treats the shared brand as insufficient for identity.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: product. Label: glass soda bottle. Verify whether the yellow-boxed product on the target and the green-boxed product on the reference are the same product.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A glass soda bottle standing upright, yellow box around it. Embossed brand script, red cap, paper label facing the camera reading Classic 330 ml.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The same bottle photographed mirrored so the label faces the other way and the cap is on the opposite side, green box around it. Same embossed script, red cap, same Classic 330 ml label where readable. Only orientation differs.

verdict.reason: Same product photographed mirrored. Orientation is not an identity signal for products, the brand script, cap color, and Classic 330 ml label all match (SOP 4.3, 8.9). Trap is to call it Different because the sides are flipped.

checklist: States that orientation or mirroring does not change product identity
Names at least one matching identity feature such as the label text, brand script, or cap
Concludes the products are the same
constraints: Reason must name a product identity feature, not a generic same product phrase (SOP 5.4)
Reason must not treat mirrored orientation as a difference (SOP 4.3)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names a matching identity feature and explicitly discounts orientation.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: location. Label: stone monument. Verify whether the yellow-boxed location on the target and the green-boxed location on the reference are the same place.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A stone monument at midday under bright sun, yellow box around the structure. Distinctive fluted columns, a carved pediment with a relief of figures, a wide stepped base. Clear skyline behind.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The same monument photographed at dusk from a different angle under warm low light, green box around the structure. Same fluted columns, same pediment relief, same stepped base. Only lighting, weather, and angle differ.

verdict.reason: Lighting, weather, and angle are not identity signals for a location. The fluted columns, pediment relief, and stepped base are stable architectural features and they all match (SOP 4.4, 8.5). Trap is to call it Different because the lighting changed, which would be a 2-star error.

checklist: Names at least two stable architectural features such as the columns, the pediment relief, or the stepped base
States the structure matches despite the lighting or angle change
Does not use lighting, weather, or angle as a deciding signal
constraints: Reason must anchor on stable architecture, not lighting or weather (SOP 4.4)
Reason must name concrete architectural features, not a generic same place phrase (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names two or more stable architectural features and discounts the lighting or angle change.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: animal. Label: brown tabby cat. Verify whether the yellow-boxed animal on the target and the green-boxed animal on the reference are the same individual animal.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A brown tabby cat sitting, yellow box around the cat. Solid white chest bib with no markings, green eyes, classic swirl tabby pattern on the flank, no collar.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A different brown tabby cat, green box around the cat. Same overall tabby coloring but the chest has a distinct dark spotted patch, amber eyes, mackerel stripe pattern rather than swirl. Clearly a different individual.

verdict.reason: Identity for an animal is the individual, not the breed. The chest marking differs, the eye color differs, and the coat pattern differs between target and reference. Same breed is not same animal (SOP 4.5, 8.7, 8.8). The conflicting markings are clearly visible, so Definitely Different.

checklist: Names at least one individual marking such as the chest pattern, eye color, or coat pattern as conflicting
States that shared breed or coloring does not make them the same animal
Concludes the animals are different individuals
constraints: Reason must use individual markings, not breed, as the deciding signal (SOP 4.5)
Reason must name a concrete marking, not a generic same dog or same cat phrase (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names a conflicting individual marking and explicitly rejects breed similarity as identity.

meta.scenario_type: animal individual markings conflict same breed

inputs.instruction: Entity type: information. Label: storefront sign text. Verify whether the yellow-boxed text on the target and the green-boxed text on the reference are the same information.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A storefront sign, yellow box around the text which reads CORNER BAKERY in a bold serif font on a dark green board.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A printed flyer, green box around the text which reads CORNER BAKERY in a thin sans-serif font in black on white. Same words, different font and layout.

verdict.reason: For information, the text content is the identity. Both read CORNER BAKERY. Different font and layout do not change the information (SOP 4.6). The text is clearly legible in both, so Definitely Same.

checklist: States that the text content matches
Notes that the font or layout difference does not affect information identity
Concludes the information is the same
constraints: Reason must decide on text content, not font or layout (SOP 4.6)
Reason must name the matching text rather than a generic same phrase (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason confirms the text content matches and discounts the font or layout difference.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: clothing. Label: hoodie with chest print. Verify whether the yellow-boxed garment on the target and the green-boxed garment on the reference are the same garment.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A grey hoodie worn by a model, yellow box around the garment. A large chest print is visible but the wearer's crossed arms fold and obscure the center of the print, so its exact graphic cannot be fully read. Cut, color, and cuffs are clear.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The same grey hoodie laid flat, green box around the garment. The chest print is fully visible, a mountain graphic. Same cut, color, cuffs, and drawcords. The target may well be the same garment but its print is partly hidden.

verdict.reason: Cut, color, cuffs, and drawcords match, but the target print is folded and cannot be confirmed. Do not penalize the garment for what is not visible, lean on the features you can see and downgrade confidence to Likely rather than Definitely (SOP 11.1 print folded row, 5.2).

checklist: Names matching visible features such as cut, color, cuffs, or drawcords
Acknowledges the print is folded or obscured and cannot be confirmed
Explains that the obscured print is why confidence is Likely rather than Definitely
constraints: Reason must not claim the print matches when it is not visible (SOP 11.1)
Reason must tie the Likely confidence to the limited visibility, not to a guess (SOP 5.2)
reason.pass_condition: The reason matches on visible construction, flags the obscured print, and justifies Likely over Definitely.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: person. Label: man holding phone. Verify whether the yellow-boxed face on the target and the green-boxed face on the reference are the same person.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A screenshot of a man holding a phone, yellow box around his face. Visible faint diagonal watermark text repeating across the whole image.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: The exact same screenshot used again as the reference, green box around the same face, the same watermark repeating across it. Target and reference are the same image.

verdict.reason: Two broken-input signals fire at once. Target and reference are the same image, and the image carries a watermark. A photo cannot verify itself and watermarked images must be flagged. This is a Flag, not a Definitely Same (SOP 5.3, 8.2).

checklist: Identifies that target and reference are the same image or that a watermark is present
States the input is broken and should be flagged rather than verified
Does not return a same or different identity verdict as if the comparison were valid
constraints: Reason must name the broken-input condition, the same image or the watermark (SOP 5.3)
Reason must not treat the item as a normal verification (SOP 8.2)
reason.pass_condition: The reason names the same-image or watermark defect and justifies a Flag rather than an identity verdict.

inputs.instruction: Entity type: clothing. Label: sleeve embroidery. Verify whether the yellow-boxed region on the target and the green-boxed garment on the reference are the same garment. The yellow box is around the sleeve only.

media.label: Target (yellow box)
media.placeholder: A navy jacket, yellow box around the right sleeve only. The sleeve shows a plain ribbed cuff and a small embroidered anchor near the wrist. A bold chest print is visible on the jacket but sits well outside the yellow box.

media.label: Reference (green box)
media.placeholder: A navy jacket, green box around the whole garment. Same ribbed cuff and the same embroidered anchor near the wrist. The chest area is plain with no print. Same cut and color.

verdict.reason: Only the area inside the yellow box is being verified, the sleeve. The ribbed cuff and embroidered anchor match. The chest print sits outside the yellow box and must be ignored, so it does not create a conflict (SOP 11.1 print outside box). The in-box features clearly match, so Definitely Same.

checklist: Limits the judgment to the sleeve inside the yellow box
Names the matching in-box features such as the cuff or the embroidered anchor
States that the chest print outside the box is not part of this verification
constraints: Reason must verify only the boxed region and ignore content outside the box (SOP 11.1)
Reason must name the in-box matching features, not a generic same jacket phrase (SOP 5.4)
reason.pass_condition: The reason confines itself to the boxed sleeve, names matching in-box features, and explicitly disregards the out-of-box print.

meta.scenario_type: clothing feature outside the box ignored

flags: Verdict option Likely Different has no dedicated item in this 12-item bank. It is the closest cousin to Skip and Likely Same and was deprioritized for count. Add one item, for example a person with a plausible but slightly conflicting jaw or hairline under decent visibility, to cover it before going live.

Entity type style is not exercised by a dedicated item in this bank. Add one style item, for example two different artworks in the same minimalist line-drawing tattoo style keyed Definitely Same, to cover all seven types.

All media are real_asset with empty url. This task keys on fine perceptual detail such as logo position and individual animal markings, which generated images cannot guarantee, so no images were generated. The data team must attach real image pairs that match each placeholder before this bank goes live, and human key review must confirm each pair supports its keyed verdict.
No new-skill proposed additions. Every competency this SOP exercises is covered by the genesis skillset S1 to S10.

worker_grading_rubric.summary: A worker is scored on verdict correctness and reason specificity together, using the right identity criteria for the entity type and the right confidence level.

dimensions.name: Verdict
dimensions.good: Correct verdict using the identity criteria for the entity type, with the right confidence level, and a Skip or Flag chosen instead of guessing when appropriate.
common_mistakes: Using outfit or background to decide a person match
Calling a location different because lighting changed
Treating same breed as same animal
Choosing Definitely when only Likely is supported
Returning an identity verdict on a broken input that should be flagged

dimensions.name: Reason
dimensions.good: Names two or more specific identity features anchored in the criteria for that entity type, so a reviewer could infer the verdict from the features alone.
common_mistakes: Generic phrases such as looks the same or same person
Naming a non-identity signal such as the outfit or the lighting
Claiming a feature matches when it is not clearly visible

free_text_rubric.strong: Same logo placement, stitching, and fabric texture match.

worker_grading_rubric.pass_threshold: Target the 5-star tier from SOP 9, correct verdict plus a reason naming at least two specific identity features. The platform applies the numeric cut score.

skillset.name: Instruction Reading
skillset.description: Read the entity type and label first and load the identity criteria that apply to that type before judging the images.

skillset.name: Attention to Detail
skillset.description: Notice the small construction or marking detail, a strap width, a logo position, a jersey number, a chest marking, that decides identity.

skillset.name: Calibrated Judgment
skillset.description: Choose definitely only when the deciding feature is clearly visible, drop to likely under doubt, and skip a genuine coin flip rather than guess.

skillset.name: Objectivity
skillset.description: Judge only on identity signals for the entity type and ignore non-identity content such as outfit, background, lighting, weather, and breed.

skillset.name: Identity Criteria Knowledge
skillset.description: Know what establishes identity for each entity type, face for a person, construction for a garment, individual markings for an animal, structure for a location.

skillset.name: Comparison
skillset.description: Weigh the target against the reference feature by feature and decide whether the identity features match or conflict.

skillset.name: Difference Detection
skillset.description: Spot the specific feature that conflicts between target and reference, such as a moved logo or a number present in one image and absent in the other.

skillset.name: Visual Quality Assessment
skillset.description: Judge whether a deciding feature is clearly visible or too occluded, distant, folded, or low resolution to support a confident call.

skillset.name: Data Integrity Screening
skillset.description: Recognize a broken input that must be flagged rather than verified, such as the same image on both sides, a watermark, a wrong box, or a same-photoshoot pair.

skillset.name: Clear Writing
skillset.description: Write a short reason that names the specific identity features used, so a reviewer can audit the decision from the features alone.
